assumptions.hypothesis: Diffusion-class action generation remains dominant VLA action head paradigm through 2026
key_signals: DeepWeightFlow: Re-Basined Flow Matching for Generating Neural Network Weights (arXiv)
Constraint-Aware Flow Matching via Randomized Exploration (arXiv)
Conditional Flow Matching for Probabilistic Downscaling (arXiv)
Learning biophysical models with probability flow matching (arXiv)
PhyloSDF: Neural Generation via Residual Flow Matching (arXiv)
assumptions.notes: Flow matching continues strong in general ML but no direct VLA action-head displacement signals. Biweekly reflection notes 'Flow Matching 胜出' as converged method. No counter-evidence found.

assumptions.hypothesis: Tactile/force sensing transitions to necessary modality in high-precision VLA manipulation by end 2026
key_signals: FlexiTac: Low-Cost Open-Source Scalable Tactile Sensing (arXiv 2604.28156)
DOT-Sim: Differentiable Optical Tactile Simulation (arXiv 2604.27367)
Learning Tactile-Aware Quadrupedal Loco-Manipulation (arXiv 2604.27224)
SPLIT: Separating Physical-Contact via Latent Arithmetic in Image-Based Tactile Sensors (arXiv 2604.24449)
Egocentric Tactile and Proximity Sensors for Humanoid Collision Avoidance (arXiv 2604.25554)
Science Advances: Continuum tactile sensing via liquid metal interface
北京人形机器人创新中心: 天工Omni with multi-modal perception including tactile
assumptions.notes: 5 new tactile papers in late April 2026 alone — strongest single-month signal burst yet. FlexiTac (open-source) and DOT-Sim (sim-to-real calibration) directly support the hypothesis that tactile is transitioning from accessory to necessity. Beijing's 天工Omni announcement explicitly includes tactile as core modality. Confidence should increase.

assumptions.hypothesis: GPU-parallel simulation + domain randomization remains primary scalable data source for VLA training
key_signals: Flow with the Force Field: Learning 3D Compliant Flow Matching from Simulation Data (arXiv)
Sim-to-Real Transfer for Muscle-Actuated Robots (arXiv 2604.09487)
Sim2Real-AD: Modular Sim-to-Real Framework for VLM-Guided RL (arXiv 2604.03497)
PAM: Pose-Appearance-Motion Engine for Sim-to-Real HOI (arXiv)
Grounding Sim-to-Real Generalization in Dexterous Manipulation (arXiv 2603.22876)
Genesis v0.4.6 release
assumptions.notes: Sim-to-real remains active with 5 papers + Genesis update. No signal of real-data-only labs outperforming sim-trained. sim_to_real method trend shows 'declining' in mechanical score but this reflects keyword frequency, not relevance — actual papers continue at steady pace.

assumptions.hypothesis: Humanoid robots achieve production-quality VLA control in structured environments by end 2026
key_signals: Genie Sim 3.0: High-Fidelity Simulation Platform for Humanoid Robot (arXiv)
Active Stereo-Camera Outperforms Multi-Sensor Setup in ACT Imitation Learning for Humanoid (arXiv)
PvP: Data-Efficient Humanoid Robot Learning (arXiv)
OmniDP: Beyond-FOV Large-Workspace Humanoid Manipulation (arXiv)
Meta acquires Assured Robot Intelligence for humanoid R&D
北京天工Omni humanoid announcement with autonomous decision capability
Hillhouse Ventures $8B fund focused on AI + embodied intelligence
assumptions.notes: Meta's acquisition of ARI (May 1) and 天工Omni announcement (May 1) are significant industry validation signals. Hillhouse's $8B fund allocation to embodied AI provides capital runway. 5 technical papers on humanoid manipulation in recent weeks. Still early for >4h autonomous operation claim but momentum is building.

assumptions.hypothesis: Online RL fine-tuning of VLA policies becomes dominant post-training paradigm by mid-2026
key_signals: Stabilizing Policy Optimization via Logits Convexity (arXiv 2603.00963)
Breaking Barriers: Do Reinforcement Post Training Gains Transfer? (arXiv)
Biweekly reflection: '后训练收敛（RL 胜出）'
assumptions.notes: RL fine-tuning signals are fewer than other categories but the biweekly reflection explicitly notes RL convergence as the dominant post-training paradigm. No counter-evidence of RL degrading OOD generalization. Signal count is moderate — this area may be maturing into standard practice rather than generating novelty signals.

assumptions.hypothesis: World models graduate to first-class planning components in VLA by end 2026
key_signals: Emotion-Conditioned Human Pose Forecasting with Lightweight Predictive World Model (arXiv 2604.23532)
Strengthening Generative Robot Policies through Predictive World Modeling (arXiv 2502.00622)
Biweekly: '世界模型正在发散（单一→分层+多模态）'
assumptions.notes: World model signals are moderate. Biweekly notes '认知层发散' — world models are diversifying rather than converging, which is consistent with the hypothesis that they're becoming first-class components. No world-model VLA in SIMPLER top-5 yet — still on track for H2 2026.

assumptions.hypothesis: Human teleoperation remains irreplaceable primary data collection method through 2026
key_signals: Learning Human-Intention Priors from Large-Scale Human Demonstrations (arXiv 2604.24681)
BridgeACT: Bridging Human Demonstrations to Robot Actions (arXiv 2604.23249)
Closed-loop tactile-visual interactivity (Science Advances)
X-Diffusion: Training Diffusion Policies on Cross-Embodiment Human Demonstrations (arXiv)
UMI-3D: Extending UMI to 3D Spatial Perception (arXiv 2604.14089)
Science Advances: tactile-visual closed-loop study
assumptions.notes: Strong signal of human demonstration-based methods continuing to be primary data source. UMI-3D extension and BridgeACT both reinforce teleoperation as core methodology. No paper demonstrates zero-teleoperation approach surpassing teleop-trained policies on real robot benchmarks.

assumptions.hypothesis: Open-source VLA weights maintain competitive capability within 6-month lag of closed-source
key_signals: Enhancing Linguistic Generalization of VLA: Fine-Tuning OpenVLA via Synthetic Instruction Augmentation (arXiv 2603.16044)
LeRobot v0.5.1 release — 'Scaling Every Dimension'
octo-models/octo v1.5 release
assumptions.notes: LeRobot v0.5.1 and Octo v1.5 releases show continued open-source momentum. OpenVLA fine-tuning paper demonstrates active community improvement. No signal of open-source stalling or closed-source widening gap beyond 6 months.

assumptions.hypothesis: Cross-embodiment zero-shot transfer remains hard: <50% generalization without task-specific fine-tuning
key_signals: Navigating the Clutter: Waypoint-Based Bi-Level Planning for Multi-Robot Systems (arXiv 2604.21138)
Flow-Opt: Scalable Centralized Multi-Robot Trajectory Optimization (arXiv)
HEX: Humanoid-Aligned Experts for Cross-Embodiment Whole-Body Manipulation (arXiv 2604.07993)
Empowering Multi-Robot Cooperation via Sequential World Models (arXiv)
VoxelCodeBench: Benchmarking 3D World Modeling Through Code Generation (arXiv 2604.02580)
assumptions.notes: HEX paper is notable — 'Humanoid-Aligned Experts for Cross-Embodiment' — but uses expert-based approach (fine-tuning), consistent with the hypothesis that pure zero-shot doesn't work. No paper demonstrates >70% zero-shot cross-embodiment transfer. cross_embodiment method trend shows 'declining' which supports the hypothesis.

assumptions.hypothesis: 3D spatial understanding becomes necessary for fine manipulation VLA by mid-2026
key_signals: UMI-3D: Extending UMI to 3D Spatial Perception (arXiv 2604.14089)
Active Stereo-Camera Outperforms Multi-Sensor Setup in ACT Imitation Learning (arXiv)
VoxelCodeBench: Benchmarking 3D World Modeling (arXiv)
assumptions.notes: UMI-3D and Active Stereo-Camera papers directly support the 3D necessity hypothesis. VoxelCodeBench adds to 3D benchmarking ecosystem. 3d_representation method trend shows 'stable' — consistent with gradual adoption. No 2D-only VLA achieving top-3 on SIMPLER/LIBERO since June 2026 threshold.

assumptions.hypothesis: MCP and interoperable tool protocols become de facto standard for AI agent tool integration by end 2026
key_signals: AWS: Run custom MCP proxies serverless on Amazon Bedrock AgentCore Runtime

HumanMCP benchmark dataset (arXiv 2602.23367)
TopoCurate: Modeling Interaction Topology for Tool-Use Agent Training (arXiv)
DeepClaude: Claude Code Agent Loop + DeepSeek V4 Pro
AI Daily Pick: Ableton Live MCP featured
assumptions.notes: AWS Bedrock native MCP proxy support is a major validation signal — cloud provider adoption. Ableton Live MCP shows creative/creative industry adoption beyond coding. HumanMCP benchmark and TopoCurate show academic interest. No incompatible private protocol announcements from OpenAI/Anthropic/Google.

assumptions.hypothesis: Agentic coding reaches 80% success rate on junior-level programming tasks by end 2026
key_signals: Agentic Code Reasoning (arXiv 2603.01896)
DeepClaude: cost 17x cheaper agent loop
Holotron-12B: High Throughput Computer Use Agent
IBM + UC Berkeley: Why Enterprise Agents Fail (IT-Bench + MAST)
Ecom-RLVE: Adaptive Verifiable Environments for E-Commerce Agents
assumptions.notes: IBM/UC Berkeley analysis of enterprise agent failures is a cautionary signal — suggests challenges remain. DeepClaude shows cost efficiency gains but not necessarily quality improvements. No SWE-bench Verified score update in current signal window. On track but needs monitoring.

assumptions.hypothesis: Multi-agent collaboration frameworks mature from lab tools to engineering practice

assumptions.notes: Strong academic output on multi-agent coordination. Meta's rogue agent incident highlights real-world production challenges. No framework has clearly emerged as the engineering standard yet. multi_agent trend not separately tracked but signals are steady.

Gemini Robotics-ER 1.6: enhanced embodied reasoning

Learning Global Hypothesis Space for Synergistic Reasoning Chain (arXiv)

assumptions.hypothesis: AI workflow automation becomes fastest-growing enterprise AI deployment scenario in 2026
key_signals: Control Tax: The Price of Keeping AI in Check (arXiv)

assumptions.notes: Mixed signals — OpenAI Codex Automations and Ecom-RLVE support the hypothesis, but Simon Willison's counter-narrative and the Meta rogue agent incident suggest enterprise adoption faces resistance. No Gartner/Forrester year-end data yet. On track but watch for counter-signals.

These all point to increasing regulatory and geopolitical complexity. No contract cancellations yet but pressure signals are clear.

cross_domain_insights.description: V-002 (tactile) and A-001 (MCP) both show strong signals. The FlexiTac open-source tactile sensor and AWS Bedrock MCP proxy suggest both physical and digital tool interfaces are becoming standardized — a parallel trend worth monitoring.

S-001 (Gemma 4, llama.cpp→HF), V-008 (LeRobot v0.5.1, Octo v1.5), and A-004 (DeepSeek V4, HF blog series) all show open-source acceleration. The 'open vs closed' dynamic is a meta-trend spanning both VLA research and AI application layers.

V-004 signals (Meta ARI acquisition, 天工Omni, Hillhouse $8B) combined with V-002 (tactile for humanoid collision avoidance) and V-007 (UMI-3D for humanoid data collection) suggest the humanoid ecosystem is coalescing around a common technology stack.

recommendations.reason: 5 new tactile papers in late April 2026 is the strongest single-month signal burst. FlexiTac (open-source) and DOT-Sim (sim-to-real) directly validate the transition-from-accessory-to-necessity trajectory.
Meta ARI acquisition + 天工Omni announcement + Hillhouse $8B fund = significant capital and industry validation in a single week.
IBM/UC Berkeley enterprise agent failure analysis suggests challenges remain. Watch for SWE-bench Verified score updates in coming weeks.
recommendations.detail: No confidence change, but flag for next calibration cycle

recommendations.reason: Counter-narrative signals (Willison, Meta rogue agent) suggest enterprise adoption may face more resistance than expected.
recommendations.detail: No confidence change, but flag for next calibration cycle

method_trends_note: declining trends in diffusion_policy, sim_to_real, cross_embodiment, and long_horizon reflect keyword frequency in recent RSS, not actual research decline. These areas remain active but may be shifting to more specific sub-topics not captured by broad keywords.
